text:
NWSL





Lots going on. Let’s get right into it. I’mEmily Olsenhere withMeg LinehanandSteph Yang— welcome to Full Time!

This is the digital version of Full Time. If you want it earlier, start every week with Full Time delivered to your inbox.Sign up here.

Those of us who stayed up last night were given a show of squad cohesion, offensive pressure and a long-range golazo. The problem for USWNT fans is that they came from the Mexican national team instead of the U.S. Having likely studied film from USWNT’s match against Argentina last week, Mexico came out with energy and pressure that the U.S. could not match andtopped Group A with a 2-0 winover the U.S. Here’s how it went down:

¡Un GO-LA-ZO de Pelayo sella el paso a los Cuartos de Final para México! 💥pic.twitter.com/qy2C6vfseN

— W Gold Cup (@GoldCup)February 27, 2024



With two wins, one loss and a seven-goal differential, theUSWNT qualifies for the quarterfinals as second-place finishers in Group A, but points and goal differential matter as teams are re-seeded ahead of the next round.

Advertisement

The final places for the quarterfinals will be decided on Tuesday when Colombia faces Puerto Rico and Brazil faces Panama, and also on Wednesday when Canada goes against Costa Rica and Paraguay takes on El Salvador. The top two teams from each group advance, plus the next highest third-place teams. Canada and Brazil have qualified already.

The USWNT now has a few days before they know their next opponent and almost a full week until they play again.

The U.S. found itself on the wrong side of the scoreboard and the record books Monday night:

Execution errors were happening everywhere for the U.S. Mexico could have won this match 4-0 if not for the crossbar and then a pair of strong saves from Alyssa Naeher late in the second half. It begs the question, could other teams find the same crack in the U.S. armor with long-range chances?

Occupational hazard 💀pic.twitter.com/LqXSq6uPP9

— Attacking Third (@AttackingThird)February 27, 2024



How much can we read into starting XIs and playing time? The answer for me is still:“Not much at all.”But so you can see the three games side-by-side, here are the line-ups…



Rotation was promised by Kilgore, and she delivered. With the media after Monday’s game, she bristled a bit at a question about whether that rotation had backfired.

“The whole group is prepared to play,” she answered. “The whole group was prepared to play tonight. We could have gone with several options, and this was the group that we chose. I’m very confident that the group is capable of executing.”

Kilgore said that it was important for all players to have opportunities in this tournament, but also important for the team to execute.

“It’s not just about partnerships, it’s about systems, roles and responsibilities,” she said.

Advertisement

From my vantage point outside the privileged bubble of the USWNT technical staff, the Gold Cup still feels like the right place for experimentation, evaluation and rotation. But if you’re going to do it, you have to actually commit.

All 14NWSLteams will have two fresh looks this season, withNike providing each club with new home and away kits. While we got a sneak peek of some during preseason (and through EA Sports FC), the league has officially released the full collection of kits … and not everything wowed us.

“The thoughtful designs reflect each club’s identity, provide a sense of joyful expression, and celebrate the energy created by the sport for the teams and their communities,” according to NWSL. “While the primary kits are where the individual club’s unique stories come to life, the secondary kits, which feature a gradient shift from dark to light using club colors, speak to the strength of the collective.”



The good:No more white shorts! Once again, women’s leagues are leading the way in innovation (ahem,MLB).

The bad:Every team’s secondary kit might as well be pulled from a variety pack of Hanes T-shirts.

My favorite (in a size large, thank you):Orlando Pride.

The league promises replica goalkeeper jerseys later this spring, too, perhaps a sign thatMary Earpshas made a global impact.

Meg, Steph and Brooks Peckbreak down the entire collection into “Good,” “Okay,” and “Yuck.”

Analytics are becoming a necessity in the NWSL.Steph Yang spoke to various data analysts and general managersaround the league about the information available now and what they hope to see in the future.

What became clear is that any club with serious championship aspirations will have to make data analytics a staffed-out, full-time department, whether it’s to gain in-game advantages or to find the best players. Those who can’t or won’t fill such a position will lag behind.

Advertisement

“I would love this to be the most technologically advanced league where we’re just recruiting all these really talented people that wouldn’t ordinarily choose to work in sports but find this to be an interesting opportunity,” said Bay FC director of data analytics Arielle Dror.

But it also comes with a balance.

“I don’t think you can Moneyball the NWSL in the way that you can baseball,” said Orlando Pride general manager Haley Carter. “It’s important that you codify it and you have a process that’s repeatable and scalable, but you also have to be cognizant of biases that are inherently built in by codifying and formalizing a process.”

As someone who lives in a city that overcharges for parking and tickets at the smallest inconvenience, I feel slightly desensitized to astronomical parking prices. But even I had a little sticker shock atKC Current’s $800 season parking feeat the new stadium.

In an email to season ticket holders, the club revealed that on-site parking would run $50 per match, plus taxes and fees. To park at all of theCurrent’s 13 regular season home games – plus one yet-to-be-announced home game as part of the NWSL’s intended international tourney during the Olympic break – fans would need to pay $700 for the year, before taxes and fees. And you know those taxes and fees aren’t cheap.



Total price for full-season parking including taxes and fees:

In a different email to members, the Current offered a $10 per car credit to use inside CPKC stadium for those who carpooled with four or more fans.

Back on the job:FormerPortland ThornsbossRhian Wilkinsonhas been appointed the new head coach of the Wales Women’s national team. The role marks Wilkinson’s return to management for the first time since resigning from the Thorns in December 2022 after she was investigated and cleared over her conduct.

Advertisement

Money, Money, Money:In the past 18 months, thewomen’s transfer record fee has been broken three times. England midfielderKeira Walshmoved from Manchester City to Barcelona for £350,000, and then Chelsea paid £385,000 for Levante’sMayra Ramirez. It was a deal that stood until Bay FC forked out £630,000 to add Zambia international forwardRacheal Kundananjito its team from another Spanish club, Madrid CFF. Is this a booming trend or something more of a warning?

Bonne chance:World Cup winnerSpainis headed to the Olympics for the first time. Goals fromJenni Hermoso,Aitana BonmatiandOna Batllein the 3-0 win over the Netherlands on Friday secured the team’s place in Paris this summer.
